skill.name: claude-settings-audit
skill.description: Audits a repository and recommends Claude Code settings.json permissions for read-only project inspection commands.
skill.summary: Generate cautious Claude Code permission recommendations from repository structure and detected tools.
skill.author: getsentry

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many external command patterns, but most are documentation examples for Claude Code permission strings rather than executable code. No prompt injection or confirmed malicious intent was found, but the skill recommends broad read-only shell and API permissions that can expose repository metadata, existing Claude settings, MCP configuration, and environment-backed service tokens.

medium_findings.title: Broad Read-Only Shell Permission Recommendations
medium_findings.description: The skill recommends broad Claude Code Bash permissions such as cat, find, gh api, Docker inspection commands, and Terraform state listing. These are framed as read-only, but they can reveal sensitive project files, repository metadata, infrastructure resource names, or remote service data when copied into settings.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The permission examples are explicit and semantically grant broad command access. The commands are not malicious by themselves, but the risk is real when used as allow-list entries.

medium_findings.title: MCP Configuration May Reference Secret Environment Variables
medium_findings.description: The skill suggests MCP server configurations that reference SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN and LINEAR_API_KEY. The examples do not print the secrets, but enabling these servers gives tools access to external services using local credentials.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The environment variable names and MCP command examples are present in the skill text. The risk depends on user installation and runtime configuration, so this is medium rather than high.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The scanner flagged weak cryptography patterns, but the referenced text is ordinary prose or identifiers such as Claude Code, a skill name, and documentation domains. No hashing, encryption, or cryptographic API usage was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line-level context shows these are text matches, not cryptographic operations. There is no executable cryptography code in the skill file.

low_findings.title: Filesystem Access Is Documentation-Only But Sensitive
low_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to read repository files and existing configuration files. This is expected for an audit skill, but users should review output before accepting recommended permissions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The filesystem commands are visible and align with the stated audit purpose. They remain relevant because configuration files can contain sensitive paths or tokens.

dangerous_patterns.title: Wildcard Bash Allow-List Entries
dangerous_patterns.description: The recommended permission strings use wildcard arguments for several commands. Wildcards make permissions easier to use but harder to constrain to safe files or read-only API calls.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The wildcard pattern is explicit in the permission examples and explained by the skill. The skill warns against mutating commands, but the broad match still creates exposure risk.

dangerous_patterns.title: External MCP Server Launch Suggestions
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill suggests uvx and npx MCP server launch commands for Sentry and Linear. These are legitimate integrations, but users should verify package provenance and credential scope before enabling them.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands and token references are present. The risk is conditional because the skill only suggests configuration and does not execute these commands directly.

content.value_statement: Teams need Claude Code permissions that fit each repository without allowing unnecessary commands. This skill reviews project structure and proposes settings for read-only inspection workflows.
actual_capabilities: Detects common language, package manager, build, infrastructure, and monorepo files.
Recommends Claude Code settings.json allow entries for read-only shell commands.
Tailors package manager commands to detected lock files and project tools.
Suggests WebFetch domains for detected frameworks and Sentry projects.
Suggests Sentry and Linear MCP configuration when related project signals exist.
Provides merge guidance when existing Claude settings are present.
limitations: It does not prove that every suggested command is safe for every repository.
It relies on visible files and may miss tools configured outside the repository.
It does not validate package provenance for suggested MCP servers.
It can expose metadata from local configuration files during analysis.

use_cases.title: Set up a new repository
use_cases.description: Create an initial Claude Code permission set based on actual project files and tools.

use_cases.title: Review existing permissions
use_cases.description: Compare current Claude settings with a read-only baseline and identify permissions to remove or narrow.

use_cases.title: Prepare team onboarding
use_cases.description: Document safe inspection commands and service integrations before enabling AI coding assistants.

prompt_templates.title: Basic repository audit
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a single repository
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this repository and recommend Claude Code settings.json permissions for read-only inspection.

prompt_templates.title: Existing settings review
prompt_templates.scenario: Check current Claude settings before sharing them with a team
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the existing Claude settings in this repository and explain which permissions should stay, change, or be removed.

prompt_templates.title: Monorepo permission plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Generate scoped recommendations for a repository with several packages
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this monorepo and recommend read-only Claude Code permissions for each detected language, package manager, and build tool.

prompt_templates.title: Security-focused configuration audit
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review before publishing team defaults
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this repository for Claude Code permissions, highlight data exposure risks, and produce conservative settings with clear exclusions.

output_examples.input: A Python service with poetry.lock, Dockerfile, Makefile, and Sentry dependencies.
output_examples.output: The skill reports Python, Poetry, Docker, Make, and Sentry. It recommends read-only inspection commands and Sentry documentation access.

output_examples.input: A Node.js workspace with pnpm-lock.yaml, turbo.json, and React dependencies.
output_examples.output: The skill identifies a pnpm monorepo with Turbo and React. It recommends pnpm inspection commands and React documentation access.

output_examples.input: A repository that already has Claude settings and an MCP configuration file.
output_examples.output: The skill summarizes existing permissions, suggests merge changes, and flags MCP entries that require credential review.

best_practices: Review every recommended permission before adding it to shared Claude Code settings.
Prefer the narrowest package manager and framework commands that match detected files.
Keep MCP credentials scoped, rotated, and documented outside the settings file.
anti_patterns: Do not copy broad command permissions into sensitive repositories without review.
Do not enable package managers or MCP servers that the repository does not use.
Do not allow build, install, deploy, write, or delete commands as read-only defaults.

faq.question: Does this skill execute commands automatically?
faq.answer: It instructs the assistant to inspect repository files and command output. Users should approve tool use according to their environment policy.

faq.question: Is the generated settings file safe to copy directly?
faq.answer: Treat it as a recommendation. Review each permission against your repository, secrets policy, and team workflow.

faq.question: Why does it include package manager commands?
faq.answer: Package manager inspection commands help Claude understand dependencies without installing or changing packages.

faq.question: Can it configure MCP servers?
faq.answer: It can suggest Sentry or Linear MCP configuration when related signals are detected. It does not verify package trust or credential scope.

faq.question: Does it support Codex?
faq.answer: The audit content is useful for Codex users who review Claude Code settings, but the permission format is specific to Claude Code.

faq.question: What should I check before publishing settings?
faq.answer: Check wildcard command permissions, API access, MCP credentials, and any command that could reveal sensitive local or remote data.
